github_issue.title: Pylon TUI can leave users offline after long homework IDs crash worker

release_commit.summary: Fix Pylon worker paths for long homework IDs

npm_package.name: @openagentsinc/pylon
npm_package.note: No npm republish was needed for this binary-only fix; the 0.1.14 launcher already resolves trusted pylon-v... GitHub release assets and auto-updates the TUI unless disabled.

what_changed: Long hosted homework run and assignment IDs are now hashed into bounded local path segments before creating run-root or supervisor attempt directories.
The Pylon TUI now labels managed worker exits as Needs attention instead of presenting them as normal startup/preparing state.
The root README, Pylon runbook, and npm bootstrap README now name pylon-v0.1.14 as the current recommended paid-training binary line.
notes: This release directly addresses the production screenshot where a user saw Managed earning worker exited with exit status: 1 while the homework panel still showed stale closing-out state.

The release asset currently covers darwin-arm64, which is the platform from the reported Apple M4 user screenshot. Other platforms continue to use the bootstrap's matching-asset selection or source-build fallback until their 0.1.14 assets are cut on native hosts.
